description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages testing, release, and post-release, using a Maven cache at .m2/repository keyed by the project name and defining MAVEN_CLI_OPTS, MAVEN_NO_TESTS, and MAVEN_OPTS variables. In the testing stage add three verify jobs named verify:jdk11, verify:jdk14, and verify:jdk8 that each run on the respective Maven Docker images (maven:3.6-jdk-11-slim, maven:3.6-jdk-14, maven:3.6-jdk-8-alpine), execute a before_script to create .m2/repository and write a minimal settings.xml, then run `mvn ${MAVEN_CLI_OPTS} verify` followed by an awk command to compute instruction coverage from target/site/jacoco-ut/jacoco.csv, collect JUnit XML reports from target/surefire-reports/TEST-*.xml as artifacts, publish coverage using the regex, and clean up snapshot files in an after_script; the verify:jdk11 job also expires its artifacts after 5 minutes, and all three jobs are excluded when the commit message matches `[silent-ignore]` or `[maven-release-plugin]`. The pipeline also defines a pages job in the post-release stage that runs on ruby:2.5, depends on verify:jdk11, uses a before_script to cd into docs and run `bundle install --path vendor`, caches the vendor directory under docs/vendor/, then runs `bundle exec jekyll build -d public`, moves the generated public folder and the JaCoCo site into the public directory, and finally triggers another pipeline via a curl POST to the GitLab API using $CI_JOB_TOKEN; this job only runs on the master branch and publishes the public/ directory as a pages artifact.
